<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439715 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00963: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-1 Service Taxonomy

- page_id: `254439715`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439715/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-1+Service+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:31.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Service Taxonomy View (SOV-1) specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (i.e. service interfaces), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one Service is a special type of another. Along with SOV-2, it specifies a standard library of Service specifications for an enterprise, which Service implementers are expected to conform to.

**Implementation**

SOV-1 can be represented using a SOV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-1 Service Taxonomy

**Related views**

The Services Interfaces in SOV-1 can realize Capabilities (from StV-2) and also support the Operational Activities (from OV-5).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Capability
- Expose

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SOV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439738 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00964: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-2 Service Interface Specification

- page_id: `254439738`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439738/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-2+Service+Interface+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:32.931+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the Service Interface Specification View (SOV-2) is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A Service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies the provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

**Implementation**

SOV-2 can be represented using

- A report which is automatically generated from all data
- A SOV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-2 Service Interface Specification

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Service Operation
- Service Attribute
- Service Parameter
- Service
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In this case, the architect specifies the provided interfaces. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439735 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00965: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping

- page_id: `254439735`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439735/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-3+Capability+to+Service+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:32.790+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Capability to Service Mapping View (SOV-3) depicts which services contribute to the achievement of a capability. It is in the form of a table generated from the database. If a network enabled capability is to be delivered by the orchestration of loosely couple services (i.e. a service-oriented architecture), it is important to know which services have the potential to support particular capabilities. This helps to prevent redundant services or capabilities, (except where specifically required) and what is known as stovepipe development. An SOV-3 presents a simple mapping of services to capabilities, showing which services contribute to which capability.

**Implementation**

SOV-3 can be represented using a StV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Capabilities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Expose
- Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SOV-3 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439696 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00966: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints

- page_id: `254439696`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439696/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-4a+Service+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:30.559+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the Service Constraints View (SOV-4a) is to specify constraints that apply to providers and consumers of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An SOV-4a product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of the service must conform.

**Implementation**

SOV-4a can be represented using:

- A SOV-4a table.
- A SOV-4a spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-4a Service Constraints

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Policy
- Service Interface

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Filling in SOV-4a table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An SOV-4a product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of the service must conform.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-4a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SOV-4a table.</li><li>A SOV-4a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sov_4a.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SOV-4a Service Constraints</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a198b097-c658-423d-8170-5c5d95260f39"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Policy</li><li>Service Interface</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aa583c3-0ae3-4e2c-89c8-e57d9c3c68cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Filling in SOV-4a table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439710 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00967: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-4b Service State Model

- page_id: `254439710`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439710/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-4b+Service+State+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:31.479+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the Service State Model View (SOV-4b) is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave.

As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. An SOV-4b is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

SOV-4b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-4b Service State Model

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service State Machine
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of the Service State Model View (SOV-4b) is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave.</p><p>As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. An SOV-4b is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-4b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sov_4b.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. SOV-4b Service State Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SOV-4b Service State Model</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75c54b3f-47ae-41ff-ad8e-2550694c2933"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service State Machine</li><li>Service Interface</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439705 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00968: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-4c Service Interaction Specification

- page_id: `254439705`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439705/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-4c+Service+Interaction+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:30.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the Service Interaction Specification View (SOV-4c) is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An SOV-4c product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see OV-6c). It’s purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

SOV-4c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-4c Service Interaction Specification

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interaction
- Service Message
- Service Interface
- Service Attribute

An SOV-4c product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see OV-6c). It’s purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-4c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sov_4c.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439841 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00969: (2026x) MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow

- page_id: `254439841`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439841/2026x+MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:41.448+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Service Functionality View (SOV-5) defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to OV-5 and SV-4, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform. It is especially useful during the initial exploration of the requirements for a service.

**Implementation**

SOV-5 can be represented using:

- A SOV-5 diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- A SOV-5 diagram based on the UML Clas diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Service Functionality Description

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow

**Related views**

An SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. It is equivalent in nature to the OV-5 and SV-4. It specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

An SOV-5 specifies the required functionality that an implementation of a service is expected to have – the implementation is represented in the SV-1 and SV-4.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Function
- Service Operation Action
- Service Function Action
- Function Edge
- Service Operation
- Service Parameter
- Service Interface

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Description diagram
- Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to OV-5 and SV-4, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform. It is especially useful during the initial exploration of the requirements for a service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SOV-5 diagram for the Operational Activity flows. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439613 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00970: (2026x) MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision

- page_id: `254439613`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439613/2026x+MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:25.881+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

StV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an StV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an OV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the AV-2 View).

**Implementation**

StV-1 can be represented using a StV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-1 Enterprise Vision

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

#### PANEL: Related procedure

Related procedure

- Creating StV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The purpose of an StV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an OV-1. StV-1 Enterprise Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>StV-1 Enterprise Vision</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58536d78-0d9a-4da8-a570-1884d3633b5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20791b34-2fa1-45aa-9f29-3c3529746a8b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedure</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating StV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439563 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00971: (2026x) MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `254439563`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439563/2026x+MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:20.978+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, i.e. it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises. The StV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are reference throughout one or more architectures. In addition it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and Key User Requirements (KUR). The StV-2 also provides metrics against each capability that may be used to measure successfully fielded capability.

**Implementation**

StV-2 can be represented using a StV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the old StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-2 Capability Taxonomy

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating StV-2 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The StV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are reference throughout one or more architectures. In addition it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and Key User Requirements (KUR). The StV-2 also provides metrics against each capability that may be used to measure successfully fielded capability.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>StV-2 can be represented using a StV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d21ac2a-4f03-4e37-b560-599e74de7180"><ac:rich-text-body>If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the old StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439670 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00972: (2026x) MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `254439670`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439670/2026x+MODAF.+StV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:28.725+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

StV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. StV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

**Implementation**

StV-3 can be represented using a StV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-3 Capability Phasing

**Related views**

The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (AcV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (StV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from StV-1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Related elements
- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

StV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>StV-3 can be represented using a StV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv_3.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>StV-3 Capability Phasing</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (AcV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (StV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from StV-1).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07ea6e2c-5649-4b56-b918-a7ae28dfc204"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Related elements</li><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Actual Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439663 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00973: (2026x) MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `254439663`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439663/2026x+MODAF.+StV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:28.438+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An StV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. The StV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.

**Implementation**

StV-4 can be represented using a StV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-4 Capability Dependencies

**Related views**

The Capability Dependencies (StV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an StV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the StV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the StV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (i.e. capability taxonomy).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating StV-4 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. The StV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>StV-4 can be represented using a StV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv_4.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>StV-4 Capability Dependencies</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The Capability Dependencies (StV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an StV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the StV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the StV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (i.e. capability taxonomy).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="847260cc-d8c2-4ec6-8247-6debd84712ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00449095-7638-47e9-8c65-583f4959fca0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating StV-4 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /><br /><br /><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439682 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00974: (2026x) MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

- page_id: `254439682`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439682/2026x+MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:29.603+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StV-5 defines Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping. It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. This view shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular EnterprisePhase. This view will provide a more detailed dependency analysis than is possible using StV-3. The StV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

**Implementation**

StV-5 can be represented using:

- A StV-5 table. 
 If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.
- A StV-5 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | StV-1 Enterprise Vision | Capability Phasing is defined in StV-1. |
|  | StV-2 Capability Taxonomy | Capabilities shown in StV-5 table are usually modeled in StV-2 Capability Taxonomy. |
|  | AcV-2 Programme Timelines | The timing is based on project milestones from AcV2 which indicate when capability configuration will bedelivered to an organiation. |
|  | OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart | Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating StV-5 table
- Modifying StV-5 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Deployment Milestones Creation Wizard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439675 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00975: (2026x) MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `254439675`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439675/2026x+MODAF.+StV-6+Operational+Activity+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:29.182+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

StV-6 can be represented using a StV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

**Related views**

The StV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using StVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building StV-6 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439836 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00976: (2026x) MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

- page_id: `254439836`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439836/2026x+MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:41.148+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) addresses the composition and interaction of resources. SV-1 now incorporates the human elements – Posts, Organizations and Roles. This view was previously known as the System Interface Description; the name change reflects the expanded scope of modeling in the solution space. The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2. An SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 (i.e. in a to-be architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV configurations that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the OV-2 may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders. A resource interaction is a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect.

Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the SV-2 and SV-6. Sub-resource assemblies may be identified in the SV-1 to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. The SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g. Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an OV-2 product may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in the SV-1.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

**Related views**

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).

The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The SV-1 depicts all interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. </p><p>Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the SV-2 and SV-6. The SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g. Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an OV-2 product may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in the SV-1.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>A SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. ## PAGE 00977: (2026x) MODAF. SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction Specification

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Internal Interaction Specification (SV-1) addresses the internal composition and interaction of resources.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).

The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).</p><p>The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b711eee-3b57-4aef-9130-7f1b0912fe4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Resource Interface</li><li>Resource Port</li><li>Resource Connector</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li><li>Resource Interaction</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5ed8ac3-b7d7-4e3c-871b-6f80a3936f20"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-1 diagram</li><li>Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439948 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00978: (2026x) MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

- page_id: `254439948`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439948/2026x+MODAF.+SV-10a+Resource+Constraints+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.853+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose the SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification is to specify functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the SV viewpoint). SV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the SV physical 
architecture.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

**Implementation**

SV-10a can be represented using:

- A SV-10a table.
- A SV-10a spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

**Related views**

SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-10a table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439957 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00979: (2026x) MODAF. SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

- page_id: `254439957`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439957/2026x+MODAF.+SV-10b+Resource+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:46.328+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) is a graphical method of describing a ResourceType’s (or Function’s) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the subjects will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

**Related views**

An SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439954 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00980: (2026x) MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `254439954`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439954/2026x+MODAF.+SV-10c+Resource+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:46.122+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Event-Trace Description provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation. The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating Resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

**Implementation**

SV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

You can also create SV-10c Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='MT'].

**Related views**

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) to describe the dynamic behavior of resources.

The data content of ‘messages’ that connect life-lines in an SV-10c View Product may be related, in modelling terms, with resource interactions (SV-1, 3), data flows (SV-4, 6) and data schema entities (SV-11) modeled in other views.

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Role
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resource Event-Trace Description provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439945 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00981: (2026x) MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema

- page_id: `254439945`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439945/2026x+MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.763+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-11 View defines the structure of the various kinds of system data that are utilized by the systems in the Architecture. The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. SV-11 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g. entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

****

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in SV-11. It supports the information engineering notation within this view.

Note that the Entity Relationship diagram is supported in SV-11. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

SV-11 can be represented using:

- A SV-11 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-11 Physical Schema

**Related views**

The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. An SV-11 is used to describe how information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Exchange Element
- Details
- Physical Data Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439942 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00982: (2026x) MODAF. SV-12 Service Provision

- page_id: `254439942`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439942/2026x+MODAF.+SV-12+Service+Provision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.659+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-12 defines the relationships between the Capability Configurations and Services.

**Implementation**

SV-12 can be represented using a SV-12 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Resources will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-12 Service Provision

**Related views**

The service attributes defined in SOV-1 can be given values in an SV-12 and related to the environment under which those values are true.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-12 Service Provision matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439847 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00983: (2026x) MODAF. SV-2 Resource Communications Description

- page_id: `254439847`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439847/2026x+MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Communications+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:41.841+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Systems Communications Description series of views specifies the communications networks and pathways 
that link systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The networks and pathways documented 
through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an 
Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2). The SV-2 series focuses on the physical characteristics of 
each link, to include specification of such attributes as the geographic location of network components (e.g. 
routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters). Attributes such as capacities (e.g. bandwidth, throughput), 
frequencies used, security encryption methods used, and other descriptive information are usually presented in a 
corresponding SV-6 product (though most architecture tools would prompt the architect to enter such data as the 
SV-2 views are being developed).

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Related views**

Any protocol and Standard referred to in an SV-2 diagram must be defined in the TV-1 Technical Standards View.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Protocol
- Standard
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The networks and pathways documented<br />through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an<br />Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2). Interaction Manager Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439828 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00984: (2026x) MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description

- page_id: `254439828`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439828/2026x+MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:40.416+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-2 series of views specifies the internal communications networks and pathways that link systems, and provides details regarding their configuration.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-2 Resource Communications Description

**Related views**

Any protocol and Standard referred to in an SV-2 diagram must be defined in the TV-1 Technical Standards View.

- System
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction

- Creating SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439819 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00985: (2026x) MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

- page_id: `254439819`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439819/2026x+MODAF.+SV-3+Resource+Interaction+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:39.188+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Matrix provides a tabular summary of the resource interactions specified in the SV-1 for the Architecture. An SV-3 Product allows a quick overview of all the resource interactions specified in one or more SV-1 diagrams. The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Rows and Columns in the matrix represent the Resources.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

**Related views**

SV-3 is a summary description of the resource interactions that are identified in SV-1.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-3 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439833 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00986: (2026x) MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description

- page_id: `254439833`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439833/2026x+MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:40.968+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are 
to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by 
each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition 
reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the 
allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow

You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='MT'].

**Related views**

An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).

The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Standard Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Function Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram
- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The primary purposes of SV-4 are<br />to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by<br />each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition<br />reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the<br />allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>An SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Activity diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block diagram.</li><li>A SysML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_4.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6>Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b19689-89ef-4e4e-b87f-3669ed85daee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="BPMN Process Diagram" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).</p><p>The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d689af-ccb0-46f1-b0e4-e0ef8f59eba7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Standard Operational Activity</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Is Capable Of Performing</li><li>Function Action</li><li>Function Edge</li><li>Function Parameter</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c056737-5485-461e-96a1-3c59320ca315"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</li><li>Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc1d56c6-6da4-4562-bcb2-024645fab5db"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Creation Wizard</li><li>Resource Interaction Manager Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439775 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00987: (2026x) MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `254439775`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439775/2026x+MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:35.365+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses the linkage between functions described in an SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in an OV-5. The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

**Implementation**

SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

**Related views**

An SV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an SV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an OV-5.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-5 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_5.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439769 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00988: (2026x) MODAF. SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `254439769`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439769/2026x+MODAF.+SV-6+Systems+Data+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:35.046+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- A SV-6 table.
- An SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).
- A SV-6 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Systems Data Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an OV-3.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Adding Existing Resource Interaction to SV-6 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-6 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-6 table.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">An SV-6 role-based table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b11eb514-520f-4cf6-84e4-90140cad3034"><ac:rich-text-body>A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>A SV-6 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_6.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439794 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00989: (2026x) MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix

- page_id: `254439794`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439794/2026x+MODAF.+SV-7+Resource+Performance+Parameters+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:37.118+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-7 is the Resource Performance Parameters Matrix and depicts the performance characteristics of a Functional Resource (system, role, or capability configuration). The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Functional Resources shown in the SV-1. The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix View specifies qualitative and quantitative characteristics of functional resources and the performance parameters of each resource. The performance parameters are selected by the architect and end user community.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- A SV-7 typical parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Typical table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Actual table

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification | The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources shown in the SV-1. |
|  | SV-2 Resource Communications Description | SV-7 expands on the information presented in an SV-2 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the SV-2. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- System
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Measurement Set
- Actual Property Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-7 Typical table
- Creating SV-7 Actual table
- Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table
- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439784 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00990: (2026x) MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

- page_id: `254439784`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439784/2026x+MODAF.+SV-8+Capability+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:36.415+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Capability Configuration Management view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The SV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  | The changes depicted in the SV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an AcV-2. When the AcV-2 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439951 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00991: (2026x) MODAF. SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

- page_id: `254439951`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439951/2026x+MODAF.+SV-9+Technology+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:46.019+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Technology & Skills Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

**Related views**

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Protocol
- Standard

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-9 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_9.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85d87550-b43a-4e04-9ef1-234160dcca9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Forecast</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Standard</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d161b2a1-4a94-492b-9d2a-f4a492a32d0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-9 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b975fd5-5418-466d-88b6-895f2747c97f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439935 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00992: (2026x) MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `254439935`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439935/2026x+MODAF.+TV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.352+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A TV-1 defines the technical and non-technical standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture.

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the TV-1 may document the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context.

**Implementation**

TV-1 can be represented by a TV-1 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-1 Standards Profile

**Related views**

A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. The SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-1 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

TV-1 Standards Profile" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-1 Standards Profile</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV.
