<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439877 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00993: (2026x) MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `254439877`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439877/2026x+MODAF.+TV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:44.179+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) MODAF > (2026x) MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.

A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.

A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.

One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.

**Implementation**

TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-2 Standards Forecast

**Related views**

A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-2 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.</p><p>A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.</p><p>A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-2 Standards Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="588d4794-1e31-43c5-bfa9-a4bcd20e04f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Standard</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Forecast</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5abc4ba6-7a82-4270-be6a-7903c9b6afb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating TV-2 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d6c355d-ada1-4642-b4cf-f7531d8372fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438599 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00994: (2026x) Modifying criteria through the legend

- page_id: `254438599`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438599/2026x+Modifying+criteria+through+the+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:54.630+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis > (2026x) Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['criteria-color', 'select-criteria', 'modify-criteria']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying criteria through the legend' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying criteria through the legend' space=''] through the map legend.

colorTo change the criteria color

1. Double-click the Legend. The Relation Criteria dialog opens.
2. Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.
3. In the Color list, select the desired color.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

otherTo select other criteria

1. Double-click the Legend.
2. In the Relation Criteria dialog choose the Simple Navigation property group.
3. Click to clear the Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.
4. Select the preferred criteria.
5. Click OK , when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can <ac:link ac:anchor="color"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[change the colors of the criteria relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> and <ac:link ac:anchor="other"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[select other criteria]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> through the map legend.</p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">color</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To change the criteria color</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend. The <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.</li><li>In the <strong>Color</strong> list, select the desired color.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select other criteria</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend.</li><li>In the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog choose the <strong>Simple Navigation</strong> property group.</li><li>Click to clear the <strong>Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context</strong> check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.</li><li>Select the preferred criteria.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439482 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00995: (2026x) Modifying CV-5 table

- page_id: `254439482`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439482/2026x+Modifying+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:10.912+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['remove-resources-from-cv-5', 'remove-capabilities-from-cv-5', 'modify-cv-5', 'add-capabilities-in-cv-5', 'add-resources-in-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415794279

1415794281

1415794280

**To add/ remove Capabilities in/from a CV-5 table**

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

**To add/ remove Resources in/from a CV-5 table**

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. The + and - buttons appears at the right of the cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - To add a Resource, select a Resource and click the + button . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard' space='']**Deployment Milestones** wizard opens.
  - To remove a Resource, select a Resource and click the - button.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Tables' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19589eac-5041-43b5-a47c-0964e57fc1a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415794279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e04925a5-8fe0-4363-abfc-555abe04d797"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415794281</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b10a1b-6be2-400e-847b-fa37f330c958"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415794280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>To add/ remove Capabilities in/from a CV-5 table</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to add or remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d011e40-d6f0-489d-a540-5965a4e1da96"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong>To add/ remove Resources in/from a CV-5 table</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li>The <strong>+</strong> and<strong> -</strong> buttons appears at the right of the cell.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dr_table_remove_resources.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying CV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>To add a Resource, select a Resource and click the <strong>+</strong> button . The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>To remove a Resource, select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9caf57d-acd0-4a93-a52f-8fe9ec6678ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about table management, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Tables" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438466 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00996: (2026x) Modifying Dr table

- page_id: `254438466`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438466/2026x+Modifying+Dr+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:19.697+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint > (2026x) Dr Deployment Schedule
- labels: ['add-capabilities-in-dr', 'remove-capabilities-from-dr', 'remove-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-model', 'manage-dr-table', 'modify-dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add/remove Capabilities in/from Dr table

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or to remove them from the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- The Capabilities are removed only from the table but not from the model.
- An Exhibits relationship between the context element and Capability is added/removed from the model.

To remove Resources from Dr table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource you want to remove and click the - button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']

The Resource is removed only from the table but not from the model.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from Dr table

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Remove from Table button in table toolbar.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from model

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Delete button in table toolbar.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Tables' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9ce94c72-84cd-4a6b-a155-c97b94fb303e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The Capabilities are removed only from the table but not from the model.</li><li>An Exhibits relationship between the context element and Capability is added/removed from the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove Resources from Dr table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li>Select a Resource you want to remove and click the <strong>-</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438405 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00998: (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views

- page_id: `254438405`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438405/2026x+NAF+4.0+viewpoints+and+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:10.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: ['naf-4', 'nato-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Architecture Framework (NAF) is a standard for developing Enterprise Architectures. An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a system (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

800600

#### PANEL: NAF 4.0 viewpoints

NAF 4.0 viewpoints

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a <span class="wp-glossary wpg-tooltip">system</span> (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views" ac:title="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoints-views_naf4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a8f2f393-ea58-4796-9db9-ff07eddbf5c6" ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9amaJGkw9xk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">600</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="71e38be2-9fd3-421a-82b6-e94766f454bd" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">NAF 4.0 viewpoints</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f75fe6f2-78a5-4558-a8bd-70f83ce7344e" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439586 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00999: (2026x) NATO All View Viewpoint

- page_id: `254439586`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439586/2026x+NATO+All+View+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:24.409+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e4daac0e-c219-49ec-a536-ac0d091ebc35" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438942 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01000: (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint

- page_id: `254438942`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438942/2026x+NATO+Capability+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:24.532+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling 
capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling<br />capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbfe801-97db-4028-b95f-fb30b8478177"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>  <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c662a95d-1b50-48a6-bd99-a750714426b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eaffc0e1-cfb5-4837-bc3e-4b9daf59bad7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439014 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01001: (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `254439014`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439014/2026x+NATO+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:30.355+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99b8c7c0-49af-4ecc-929f-17777e8e052a"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f9c5597d-1c9b-46ac-b11b-bb539523bda6" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438972 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01002: (2026x) NATO Programme Viewpoint

- page_id: `254438972`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438972/2026x+NATO+Programme+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:27.071+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details 
and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details<br />and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9ea9c0fe-d4ae-4812-a077-9efc19352422" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439123 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01003: (2026x) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint

- page_id: `254439123`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439123/2026x+NATO+Service-Oriented+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:39.628+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cae37911-de3e-495e-8809-d1f9df74fe9b" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439170 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01004: (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint

- page_id: `254439170`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439170/2026x+NATO+Systems+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:41.657+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.

The views of this view are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f2d8bd4-b5bf-4e5b-b114-1604a594cf3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this view are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c343f17-2404-438d-8e86-392002f17168" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439370 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01005: (2026x) NATO Technical Viewpoint

- page_id: `254439370`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439370/2026x+NATO+Technical+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.248+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d79a3e72-ee4e-4961-b192-01975c9717f7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 01006: (2026x) NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `254439868`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439868/2026x+NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:43.543+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the NAV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. NAV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the NAV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

NAV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial NAV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a NAV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439921 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01007: (2026x) NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `254439921`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439921/2026x+NAV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:44.899+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NAV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An NAV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:

- The name used for this element in the architecture
- Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names
- A brief description of the element

**Implementation**

NAV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have NAV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will bemigrated into the NAV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439883 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01008: (2026x) NCV-1 Capability Vision

- page_id: `254439883`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439883/2026x+NCV-1+Capability+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:44.291+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the NAV-2 View). It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

NCV-1 can be represented using a NCV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-1 Capability Vision

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439990 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01009: (2026x) NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `254439990`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439990/2026x+NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:48.787+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-2 Product models capability taxonomies. The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, for example, it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises.

**Implementation**

NCV-2 can be represented using a NCV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the oldNCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-2 diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439987 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01010: (2026x) NCV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `254439987`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439987/2026x+NCV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:48.551+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

**Implementation**

NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-3 Capability Phasing****

**Related views**

The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-3 Capability Phasing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NCV-3 Capability Phasing<strong><br /></strong></h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9afaa9f-77a4-4c7c-9c8f-acb8c1373694"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Actual Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254440000 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01011: (2026x) NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `254440000`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254440000/2026x+NCV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:49.351+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities (capability clusters). The NCV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.

**Implementation**

NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

**Related views**

The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the NCV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the NCV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (for example, capability taxonomy).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-4 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-4 Capability Dependencies</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439993 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01012: (2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping

- page_id: `254439993`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439993/2026x+NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:48.918+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-5 defines Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping. It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. The NCV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

**Implementation**

NCV-5 can be represented using:

- A NCV-5 table. 
 If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold NCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.
- A NCV-5 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Capability Phasing is defined in NCV-1. |
|  |  | Capabilities shown in NCV-5 table are usually modeled in NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy. |
|  |  | The timing is based on project milestones from NPV1 which indicate when capability configuration will be delivered to an organization. |
|  |  | Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-5 table
- Modifying NCV-5 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Deployment Milestones Creation Wizard

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="59">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Nvc_1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td>
         <ac:link>
          <ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-1 Capability Vision" />
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>Capability Phasing is defined in NCV-1.</p></td>
       </tr>
       <tr>
        <td>
         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="59">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Nvc_2_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td>
         <ac:link>
          <ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy" />
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>Capabilities shown in NCV-5 table are usually modeled in NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy.</p></td>
       </tr>
       <tr>
        <td>
         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="58">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Npv_1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td>
         <ac:link>
          <ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping" />
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>The timing is based on project milestones from NPV1 which indicate when capability configuration will be delivered to an organization.</p></td>
       </tr>
       <tr>
        <td>
         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="50">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Nov_4_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td>
         <ac:link>
          <ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" />
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p></td>
       </tr>
      </tbody>
     </table>
     <p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
    </ac:layout-cell>
    <ac:layout-cell>
     <p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439984 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01013: (2026x) NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

- page_id: `254439984`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439984/2026x+NCV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activity+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:48.202+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-6 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

NCV-6 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

**Related views**

The NCV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using NCVs and operational activities analyzed using NOVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the NSV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-6 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nvc_6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The NCV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using NCVs and operational activities analyzed using NOVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the NSV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43c13a43-5d21-45c4-997e-470b6689728a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Standard Operational Activity</li><li>Maps to Capability</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a477d4-fd48-4e64-a778-679f9a4d40fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Building NCV-6 matrix</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438944 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01014: (2026x) NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

- page_id: `254438944`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438944/2026x+NCV-7+Capability+to+Service+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:24.615+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-7 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and services as defined for SOA.

**Implementation**

NCV-7 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Capabilities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Expose
- Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-7 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Capabilities will be used as the column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="844e3ba4-5628-4e60-b061-3786cf796953"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interface</li><li>Expose</li><li>Capability</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e24c3c8c-39d2-43f6-abe2-00bba136fc29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Building NCV-7 matrix</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097310434

1097310437

1097310436

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: A needline documents the requirement to exchange information between nodes. The needline does not indicate how the information transfer is implemented.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| exchangedItem : NeedlineExchange[*] | An exchange that occurs on the Needline. |

##### Extensions

UML Association, UML Connector

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description)

[OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-3+Operational+Resource+Flow+Matrix)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439806 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01016: (2026x) New Service Channel wizard

- page_id: `254439806`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439806/2026x+New+Service+Channel+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:37.945+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['new-service-channel-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **New Service Channel** wizard supports the automated Service Oriented modeling in UPDM plugin.

On the basis of input data, the wizard:

- Creates a Service Channel and its required prerequisites.
- Synchronizes Service Parameters with Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction Items.
- Creates the SV-1 internal diagram to visualize a Service Channel and connected parties.

To open the**New Service Channel** wizard, do one of the following

- Select a Needline and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- Select a Resource Interface and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- Select a Service Channel and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- On the Service Channels Summary table toolbar, click the Add New button.
- On the Service Channels Summary matrix, click the intersection and from the shortcut menu, select New Service Channel .

Dependent on the way the wizard has been opened, it either knows about the Service Provider and the Service Receiver or does not.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

1. Service Channel specification. How to specify a Service Channel?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allows you to specify participating parties, service and request ports, and service interfaces.By default Service Provider and Service Requester are required, if you want to finish the wizard in the step #1. You can also choose an existing Service Channel, existing Service and Request points, and Service Interfaces.Service Interfaces can be left undefined. In this case you will not be allowed to proceed further, but you will be able to finish the wizard.Box nameDescriptionService ProviderPerformer/ Resource providing a service for a service requester. If Service Provider is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Provider is unknown, it can be selected from the model.Service RequesterPerformer/ Resource requesting a service from a service provider. If Service Requester is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Requester is unknown, it can be selected from the model.Service ChannelConnector expressing a communication channel between Service Provider and Service Requester. A new Service Channel can be created or the existing one can be selected. Only Service Channels connecting existing and selected Service and Request points can be selected.ServiceAn offer of the Service by Service Provider to Service Receivers. Only Services owned by Service Provider can be selected. If there are none, new Service will be created by default.RequestThe use of a Service by the Service Requester. Only Requests owned by Service Requester can be selected. If there are none, new Request will be created by default.Provider's Service InterfaceThe type of the Service port, which describes interfaces the Service port provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information provided by Service Provider.Requester's Service InterfaceThe type of the Request Point, which describes interfaces the Request Point provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information required by Requester.ButtonDescriptionSwitch Service Provider with Service RequesterThe button is visible only if both Service Provider and Service Requester are known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel).Click to switch Service Provider with Service Requester.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] (by the**Service Provider** box)The button is visible only if Service Provider is unknown.Click to open the**Select Elements** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] (by the**Service Requester** box)The button is visible only if Service Requester is unknown.Click to open the **Select Elements** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Provider's Service Interface** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog for Provider's Service Interface search/selection/creation.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Requester's Service Interface** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog for Requester's Service Interface search/selection/creation.
2. Provided Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification. How to specify a provided exchange Items?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allow you to specify provided exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.Check boxDescriptionUse Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource InteractionsIf selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.
3. Required Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification. How to specify a required exchange Items?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allow you to specify required exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.Check boxDescriptionUse Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource InteractionsIf selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones. The first step is mandatory and the other steps are optional.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Service Channel Summary Table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>New Service Channel</strong> wizard supports the automated Service Oriented modeling in UPDM plugin.</p><p>On the basis of input data, the wizard:</p><ul><li>Creates a Service Channel and its required prerequisites.</li><li>Synchronizes Service Parameters with Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction Items.</li><li>Creates the SV-1 internal diagram to visualize a Service Channel and connected parties.</li></ul><p>To open the<strong> New Service Channel</strong> wizard, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a Needline and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the <strong>New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>Select a Resource Interface and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the <strong>New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>Select a Service Channel and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the<strong> New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>On the Service Channels Summary table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>On the Service Channels Summary matrix, click the intersection and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>New Service Channel</strong>.</li></ul><p>Dependent on the way the wizard has been opened, it either knows about the Service Provider and the Service Receiver or does not.</p><p>The wizard consists of these three steps:</p><ol><li><p>Service Channel specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e70466f-6043-40c3-b96c-9ed92abf44da"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a Service Channel?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying Service Channel in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying Service Channel in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This step allows you to specify participating parties, service and request ports, and service interfaces.</p><p>By default Service Provider and Service Requester are required, if you want to finish the wizard in the step #1. You can also choose an existing Service Channel, existing Service and Request points, and Service Interfaces.</p><p>Service Interfaces can be left undefined. In this case you will not be allowed to proceed further, but you will be able to finish the wizard.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Box name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Service Provider</td><td><p>Performer/ Resource providing a service for a service requester. If Service Provider is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Provider is unknown, it can be selected from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service Requester</td><td><p>Performer/ Resource requesting a service from a service provider. If Service Requester is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Requester is unknown, it can be selected from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service Channel</td><td><p>Connector expressing a communication channel between Service Provider and Service Requester. A new Service Channel can be created or the existing one can be selected. Only Service Channels connecting existing and selected Service and Request points can be selected.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service</td><td><p>An offer of the Service by Service Provider to Service Receivers. Only Services owned by Service Provider can be selected. If there are none, new Service will be created by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Request</td><td><p>The use of a Service by the Service Requester. Only Requests owned by Service Requester can be selected. If there are none, new Request will be created by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Provider's Service Interface</td><td><p>The type of the Service port, which describes interfaces the Service port provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information provided by Service Provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Requester's Service Interface</p></td><td><p>The type of the Request Point, which describes interfaces the Request Point provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information required by Requester.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.041%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 32.9114%;" /><col style="width: 67.0886%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p>Switch Service Provider with Service Requester</p></td><td><p>The button is visible only if both Service Provider and Service Requester are known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service ri:filename="button_dots.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> (by the<strong> Service Provider</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>The class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Provider's Service Interface</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the element Selection dialog for Provider's Service Interface search/selection/creation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Requester's Service Interface</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the element Selection dialog for Requester's Service Interface search/selection/creation.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Provided Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3af2d300-fbac-473b-8772-148cf8c04291"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a provided exchange Items?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying provided exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying provided exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This step allow you to specify provided exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Use Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource Interactions</td><td>If selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Required Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="055cf5bb-a609-44a6-861a-82b11bfbacbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a required exchange Items?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying required exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying required exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) New Service Channel wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This step allow you to specify required exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Use Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource Interactions</td><td>If selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1cb7191e-e182-4554-b2ab-bda7f057ff4d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first step is mandatory and the other steps are optional.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aef28ee6-3299-48a5-b9fa-a4020ebcd43d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Service Channel Summary Table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01017: (2026x) No Longer Used Milestone

- page_id: `254438808`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438808/2026x+No+Longer+Used+Milestone
- version_number: 3

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915485182

915485185

915485184

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an ActualOrganisationResource ceased to use or is slated to cease using a CapabilityConfiguration from a specific point in time. This is used to describe capabilities going out of service with specific organizations or posts.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| noLongerUsedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource[1..*] | ActualOrganizationalResources that are no longer using CapabilityConfiguration that went out of service at this Milestone. |
| configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*] | Affected CapabilityConfigurations. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping)

[NSV-8 System Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NSV-8+System+Evolution+Description)

## PAGE 01018: (2026x) Node

- page_id: `254439489`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097338417

1097338420

1097338418

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Node (MODAF::NodeType, NAF::NodeType) is a logical entity that performs operational activities.

Nodes are specified independently of any physical realization.

DoDAF: A Node (DoDAF::OperationalNode) is an element of the operational architecture that produces, consumes, or processes information.

This is also a specialization of Performer.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

- page_id: `254439485`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097398545

1097398548

1097398547

##### Description

UPDM: A port is a property of a Node that specifies a distinct interaction point between the node and its environment or between the (behavior of the) node and its internal parts. It is the “entry/exit” point where resources (e.g., energy, information/data and people, etc.) flow in and out of a node.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)
