<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439255 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01046: (2026x) NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `254439255`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439255/2026x+NSV-5+System+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:51.456+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NATO System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (NSV-5) addresses the linkage between functions described in an NSV-4 and Operational Activities specified in an NOV-5. The NSV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

**Implementation**

NSV-5 can be represented using a NSV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

**Related views**

An NSV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an NSV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an NOV-5.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NSV-5 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The NSV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution. </p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NSV-5 can be represented using a NSV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439241 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01047: (2026x) NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `254439241`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439241/2026x+NSV-6+Systems+Data+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:49.706+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NATO Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An NSV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.

The term System Data Exchange does not refer to one NMM element. A data exchange as described in NSV-6 is a combination of a System Port Connector and the Data Elements 
that flow across it. Any properties shown in an NSV-6 table will be properties of the System Port Connector.

**Implementation**

NSV-6 can be represented using:

- A NSV-6 table.
- An NSV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).
- A NSV-6 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Systems Data Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

NSV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical NOV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an NOV-3.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Adding New Resource Interaction to NSV-6 table
- Adding Existing Resource Interaction to NSV-6 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439278 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01048: (2026x) NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description

- page_id: `254439278`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439278/2026x+NSV-7+System+Typical+Quality+Requirements+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:53.457+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NSV-7 is the System Typical Quality Requirements Matrix and depicts the performance characteristics of a Functional Resource (system, role, or capability configuration). The System Typical Quality Requirements Matrix expands on the information presented in an NSV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Functional Resources shown in the NSV-1. The System Typical Quality Requirements Matrix View specifies qualitative and quantitative characteristics of functional resources and the performance parameters of each resource. The performance parameters are selected by the architect and end user community.

**Implementation**

NSV-7 can be represented using:

- A NSV-7 typical parameters table.
- A NSV-7 actual parameters table.
- A NSV-7 actual parameters spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NSV-7 Typical table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NSV-7 Actual table

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an NSV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the NSV-1. |
|  |  | NSV-7 expands on the information presented in an NSV-2 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the NSV-2. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- System
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Measurement Set
- Actual Property Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NSV-7 Typical table
- Creating NSV-7 Actual table
- Generating NSV-7 Actual table from NSV-7 Typical table
- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439267 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01049: (2026x) NSV-8 System Evolution Description

- page_id: `254439267`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439267/2026x+NSV-8+System+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:52.724+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NATO System Evolution Description view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The NSV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NSV-8 System Evolution Description

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  | The changes depicted in the NSV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an NPV-1. When the NPV-1 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439331 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01050: (2026x) NSV-9 Technology Forecast

- page_id: `254439331`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439331/2026x+NSV-9+Technology+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:58.432+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NATO Technology Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in NSV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. NSV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The NSV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the NSV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.

**Implementation**

NSV-9 can be represented using a NSV-9 table.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NSV-9 Technology Forecast****

**Related views**

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see NSV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the NSV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (NTV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Protocol
- Standard

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NSV-9 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in NSV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. NSV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The NSV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the NSV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NSV-9 can be represented using a NSV-9 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nsv_9.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NSV-9 Technology Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NSV-9 Technology Forecast<strong><br /></strong></h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see NSV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the NSV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (NTV-2).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1ce94ba-9a95-4830-95c0-34ca1f8ba558"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Forecast</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Standard</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d894c2d5-eecf-4828-b867-37a72e4adbbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NSV-9 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f7457b6-be03-44c6-91d4-a9bf10fe9407"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439413 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01051: (2026x) NTV-1 Technical Standards Profile

- page_id: `254439413`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439413/2026x+NTV-1+Technical+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:03.791+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Technical Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A NTV-1 defines the technical and non-technical standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture.

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the NTV-1 may document the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context.

**Implementation**

NTV-1 can be represented by a NTV-1 table.

**Sample**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
NTV-1 Technical Standards Profile

**Related views**

A NTV-1 serves as the bridge between the NSV and NTV. The NSV-9 forecasts relate to the NTV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NTV-1 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The NSV-9 forecasts relate to the NTV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f008ff08-c16f-4c0b-ad1d-eb5f57f276ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Standard</li><li>Protocol</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81cfc958-9804-43f3-8719-1f5048046679"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NTV-1 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439372 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01052: (2026x) NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast

- page_id: `254439372`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439372/2026x+NTV-2+Technical+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.304+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Technical Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the NSV-8 and NSV-9 views.

A Technical Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.

A NTV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (NTV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.

One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.

**Implementation**

NTV-2 can be represented using a NTV-2 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast

**Related views**

A NTV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from NSV-1, NSV-2, NSV-4, NSV-6, and NOV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the NSV-8 and NSV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the NSV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the NTV-2. A timed technology forecast from the NSV-9 is related to a NTV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a NTV-2 standard (for example, a standard listed in NTV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NTV-2 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the NSV-8 and NSV-9 views.</p><p>A Technical Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.</p><p>A NTV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (NTV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NTV-2 can be represented using a NTV-2 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ntv_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A NTV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from NSV-1, NSV-2, NSV-4, NSV-6, and NOV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the NSV-8 and NSV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the NSV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the NTV-2. A timed technology forecast from the NSV-9 is related to a NTV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a NTV-2 standard (for example, a standard listed in NTV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11f9db41-691c-483a-86bf-62b4db20220b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Standard</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Forecast</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f76c885f-343b-40f2-a815-b0a47baa4f46"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NTV-2 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ec73876-e0a1-405c-92f8-dc778a658740"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01053: (2026x) Operational Activity

- page_id: `254439458`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439458/2026x+Operational+Activity
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:34.475+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097526922

1097526924

1097526923

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out.

DoDAF: An activity is an action performed in conducting the business of an enterprise. It is a general term that does not imply a placement in a hierarchy (e.g., it could be a process or a task as defined in other documents and it could be at any level of the hierarchy of the OV-5). It is used to portray operational actions not hardware/software system functions.

This is also a specialization of Activity.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| subject : ActivitySubject[*] | An object acting upon this OperationalActivity. |

Extensions

UML Activity

It is a general term that does not imply a placement in a hierarchy (e.g., it could be a process or a task as defined in other documents and it could be at any level of the hierarchy of the OV-5). It is used to portray operational actions not hardware/software system functions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" 1097557078

1097557080

1097557079

##### Description

UPDM: The OperationalActivityAction is defined as a call behavior action that invokes the activity that needs to be preformed.

MODAF/NAF: Used to relate an OperationalActivity to its sub-activities.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML CallBehaviorAction

1098341861

1098341863

1098341862

##### Description

UPDM: An extension of «ActivityEdge» that is used to model the flow of control/objects through an Operational Activity.

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalActivityEdge (MODAF::OperationalActivityFlow, NAF::OperationalActivityFlow) is a flow of information, energy or materiel from one activity to another.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| carriedItem : NeedlineExchangeItem[0..*] | Item that is carried on this OperationalActivityEdge. |

Extensions

UML ActivityEdge

[OV-5 Operational Activity Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model)

1098746949

1098746952

1098746951

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalEventTrace (MODAF::OperationalInteractionSpecification, NAF::OperationalInteractionSpecification) is a specification of the interactions between nodes in an operational architecture.

DoDAF: The Operational Event-Trace Description (OV-6c) DoDAF- described View provides a time ordered examination of the resource flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event- trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF

##### Extensions

UML Interaction

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439448 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 01058: (2026x) Operational Exchange

- page_id: `254439448`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439448/2026x+Operational+Exchange
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:35.779+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1098845897

1098845899

1098845898

##### Description

UPDM: An utility element used as common flow for:

- InformationExchange
- OrganizationalExchange
- EnergyExchange
- MaterielExchange
- ConfigurationExchange
- GeoPoliticalExtent

An operational exchange is formed when an activity of one operational node consumes items produced by another activity of a different operational node.

An operational exchange describes the characteristics of the exchanged item, such as the content, format (voice, imagery, text and message format, etc.), throughput requirements, security or classification level, timeliness requirement, and the degree of interoperability.

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalExchange (MODAF::LogicalFlow, NAF::LogicalFlow) asserts that a flow exists or is required between Nodes (e.g. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Information Flow

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-5 Operational Activity Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-5 Operational Activity Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Model)

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3796c80c-b338-45f1-a156-77ba6bd96260"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1098845897</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b94712be-cc61-4fad-87ea-3e62286de075"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1098845899</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05d2b2cb-04b8-4587-8bf2-5fc91f50f1dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1098845898</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span style="font-size: 14.0px;">UPDM: An utility element used as common flow for: </span></p><ul><li><span style="font-size: 14.0px;">InformationExchange</span></li><li>OrganizationalExchange</li><li>EnergyExchange</li><li>MaterielExchange</li><li>ConfigurationExchange</li><li>GeoPoliticalExtent</li></ul><p><span>An operational exchange is formed when an activity of one operational node consumes items produced by another activity of a different operational node.</span></p><p><span>An operational exchange describes the characteristics of the exchanged item, such as the content, format (voice, imagery, text and message format, etc.), throughput requirements, security or classification level, timeliness requirement, and the degree of interoperability.</span></p><p><span>MODAF/NAF: An OperationalExchange (MODAF::LogicalFlow, NAF::LogicalFlow) asserts that a flow exists or is required between Nodes (e.g. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Information Flow</span></p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model">OV-5 Operational Activity Model</a></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model">MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Model">NOV-5 Operational Activity Model</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439812 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01059: (2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `254439812`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439812/2026x+Operational+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:38.283+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['operational-exchange-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Exchange Manager dialog enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data. You can edit existing Operational Exchanges by adding new Operational Exchange Items, realizing or ignoring existing Operational Exchanges on selected relationship, specifying producing and consuming Operational Activities.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Select a Node Association, Needline, Operational Activity Edge, or Operational Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange_manager.png'] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='']Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='']
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1099138364

1099138366

1099138365

##### Description

UPDM: Message for use in an Operational Event-Trace which carries any of the subtypes of OperationalExchange. This is used to provide additional information about OperationalMessages for display on an OV-6c.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| carries : NeedlineExchange[*] | Carried Needline Exchange |

##### Extensions

UML Message

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NOV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description)

## PAGE 01063: (2026x) Operational State Description

- page_id: `254439546`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439546/2026x+Operational+State+Description
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:37.545+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1099316534

1099316536

1099316535

##### Description

UPDM: A state machine describing an operational behavior or property.

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalStateMachine (MODAF::OperationalStateDescription, NAF::OperationalStateDescription) is a rule governing an operational behavior or property.

DoDAF: The Operational State Transition Description (OV-6b) DoDAF-described View is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF

##### Extensions

UML State Machine

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-6b Operational State Transition Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-6b Operational State Transition Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-6b Operational State Transition Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NOV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438399 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01064: (2026x) Operational viewpoint

- page_id: `254438399`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438399/2026x+Operational+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:10.245+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['operational-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35835998

35836001

35836000

“DoDAF-described Models in the Operational Viewpoint describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and resource flow exchanges required to conduct operations. A pure operational model is materiel independent. However, operations and their relationships may be influenced by new technologies, such as collaboration technology, where process improvements are in practice before policy can reflect the new procedures. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

800700

35835997

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A pure operational model is materiel independent. However, operations and their relationships may be influenced by new technologies, such as collaboration technology, where process improvements are in practice before policy can reflect the new procedures. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="231e74bd-ad93-4280-84f9-eb58bcb0c3bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">700</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="467a21b3-34ea-4e1b-b51f-5b8c0dae3b80"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35835997</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6443fbc4-5cf9-4905-96cc-782d970e524c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

1338917564

1338917563

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService) is a ProjectMilestone that indicates a project's deliverable is to go out of service.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Property

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*] | CapabilityConfiguration that goes out of service at this OutOfServiceMilestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9175ab72-08e6-4a73-ace4-dabc7f048f36"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1338917562</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04879279-ed92-48e4-b99d-32aba6a03537"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1338917564</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e073a697-a62d-47a5-bc06-2f5192d96d7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1338917563</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: An OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService) is a ProjectMilestone that indicates a project's deliverable is to go out of service.</span></p><p><span>DoDAF: NA</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, DoDAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Property</span></h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*]</span></p></td><td><p><span>CapabilityConfiguration that goes out of service at this OutOfServiceMilestone.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Instance Specification</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438402 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01069: (2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `254438402`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438402/2026x+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:10.538+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic', 'ov-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35929568

35929570

35929569

**Description**

The OV-1 describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario. It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.

The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.

The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:

- Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.
- Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.
- Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- An OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- An OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A link to an external document.
- A UML Composite Structure Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

35929567

****

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) High - Level Operational Concept' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Concept Role' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Arbitrary Relationship' space='']

It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.</p><p>The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.</li><li>Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.</li><li>Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>An OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li><p>A link to an external document.</p></li><li><p>A UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" ac:alt="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcb7b26d-1164-47a0-9d7c-bed7d9d92ee5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35929567</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1c5c878e-6c1a-4bf2-985c-cc9f9b9092d2" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) High - Level Operational Concept" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[High-Level Operational Concept]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Concept Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Arbitrary Relationship" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439252 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01070: (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `254439252`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439252/2026x+OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:51.131+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description', 'ov-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

36389379

36389381

36389380

**Description**

The OV-2 DoDAF-described Model applies the context of the operational capability to a community of anticipated users. The primary purpose of the OV-2 is to define capability requirements within an operational context. The OV-2 may also be used to express a capability boundary.

New to DoDAF V2.0, the OV-2 can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. A specific application of the OV-2 is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) flows. The logical pattern need not correspond to specific organizations, systems or locations, allowing Resource Flows to be established without prescribing the way that the Resource Flows are handled and without prescribing solutions.

The intended usage of the OV-2 includes:

- Definition of operational concepts.
- Elaboration of capability requirements.
- Definition of collaboration needs.
- Applying a local context to a capability.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.
- Allocation of activities to resources.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Needlines established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] model or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''], on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or swim lanes on the activities.

36389378

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439238 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01071: (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description

- page_id: `254439238`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439238/2026x+OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:49.510+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description', 'ov-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

49817542

49817544

49817543

**Description**

A specific application of the Operational Resource Flow Internal Description is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) internal flows.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Needlines established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a SV-1 Systems Interface Description model or SvcV-1 Services Context Description model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and OV-5b Operational Activity Model are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5b, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or swim lanes on the activities.

49817541

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Node Role' space='']

## PAGE 01072: (2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `254438586`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438586/2026x+OV-3+Operational+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:51.652+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix', 'ov-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

898739316

898739318

898739317

**Description**

The OV-3 addresses operational Resource Flows exchanged between Operational Activities and locations.

Resource Flows provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on Resource Flows that cross the capability boundary.

The intended usage of the OV-3 includes definition of interoperability requirements.

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- An OV-3 table.
- An OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Node Roles (performer usages).
- An OV-3 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

898739315

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438574 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01073: (2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `254438574`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438574/2026x+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:47.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-4-organizational-relationships-chart', 'ov-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

899477282

899477285

899477283

**Description**

The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).

A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.

An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.

The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:

- Organizational analysis.
- Definition of human roles.
- Operational analysis.

The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:

- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- An OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships' space=''] where organizations own projects. In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']).

899477280

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Person Type' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Skill' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438552 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01074: (2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `254438552`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438552/2026x+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:40.286+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-5', 'ov-5a-operational-activity-decomposition-tree', 'ov-5b-operational-activity-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425992109

1425992112

1425992111

**Description**

The OV-5a and the OV-5b describe the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks); Input/Output flows between activities, and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architectural Description.

The OV-5a and OV-5b describes the operational activities that are being conducted within the mission or scenario.

The OV-5a and OV-5b can be used to:

- Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with OV-2.
- Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy.
- Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities.
- Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinized further.
- Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in the OV-6a Operational Rules Model, the OV-6b State Transition Description, and the OV-6c Event-Trace Description.

The OV-5b describes the operational, business, and defense portion of the intelligence community activities associated with the Architectural Description, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the activities.
- Resources exchanged between activities.
- External interchanges (from/to business activities that are outside the scope of the model).

An Operational Activity is what work is required, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical activities and locations in OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as System Functions (described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description) or Service Functions (described in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description) which are the how to the Operational Activities what, i.e., they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The intended usage of the OV-5a and OV-5b includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- An OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-5a.png']
- An OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-5b.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

You can also create OV-5 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin.

The OV-5s and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] model are, to a degree, complements of each other. The OV-5s focuses on the operational activities whereas [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] model focuses on the operational activities in relation to locations. Due to the relationship between locations and operational activities, these types of models should normally be developed together.

1425992107

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

291401905

291401907

291401906

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438545 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01076: (2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model

- page_id: `254438545`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438545/2026x+OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:38.602+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'ov-6a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

382653897

382653899

382653898

**Description**

An OV-6a specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.

The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6a can be represented using:

- An OV-6a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-6a.PNG']
- An OV-6a spreadsheet report.

Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.

382653896

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7c7f39c-17f8-4661-a920-b7de0d6bdb11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382653897</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac43efb6-dd93-487e-95fb-69564dea8215"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382653899</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c579dcd-9a8a-4a6f-8af6-36158d7f1789"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382653898</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An OV-6a specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Definition of business rules.</li><li>Identification of operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-6a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An OV-6a table.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" ac:alt="OV-6a Operational Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-6a.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An OV-6a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p>Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dafbdf36-9c51-4c94-a2ad-6e38bb233b6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382653896</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a564a7f8-b644-4779-a080-3823ac37b510" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Mission" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439340 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01077: (2026x) OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

- page_id: `254439340`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439340/2026x+OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:59.187+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6b-operational-state-transition-description', 'ov-6b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

382941516

382941518

382941517

**Description**

The OV-6b is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''].

382941515

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational State Description' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational State' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Performer' space='']

The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of business events.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description" ac:alt="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-6b.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6b Operational State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b764ab0-59f4-46dd-898b-715747935134"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382941515</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational State" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Performer" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438567 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01078: (2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `254438567`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438567/2026x+OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:44.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6c-operational-event-trace-description', 'ov-6c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

383506446

383506448

383506447

**Description**

The OV-6c provides a time-ordered examination of the Resource Flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The OV-6c can be used by itself or in conjunction with an OV-6b State Transition Description to describe the dynamic behavior of activities.

The intended usage of the OV-6c includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

OV-6c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The information content of messages in an OV-6c may be related with the Resource Flows in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] and information entities in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''].

383506445

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Event Trace' space='']

## PAGE 01079: (2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `254439317`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439317/2026x+OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:57.086+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

383707028

383707031

383707030

OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities. An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

383707026

**Related Pages:**

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational viewpoint' space='']

1099364048

1099364050

1099364049

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The OwnsProcess (MODAF::ProcessOwner) relationship asserts that an ActualOrganizationalResource has responsibility for an OperationalActivity.

This does not imply the resource conducts the activity, merely that it has managerial responsibility for it.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438438 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01082: (2026x) P1 Resource Types

- page_id: `254438438`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438438/2026x+P1+Resource+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:16.376+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p1-resource-types', 'p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P1 Resource Types view specifies the types of Resources, Technology and Competences required for the architecture. It can also be used to specify properties of Resources. This view collects together all the Resource Types in the architecture. It can also be used to identify Technologies and Competences and map these to resource types. Technologies and Competences can also be set against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use.

The P1 view may be used for:

- Identifying Resource Taxonomies.
- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR trends analysis.
- Recruitment planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.
- Definition of performance characteristics.
- Identification of non-functional requirements (input to SRD).

**Implementation**

The P1 view can be represented using:

- A P1 Resource Types diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P1_typical.png']
- A P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Technology Forecast table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A P1 Resource Types Service Provision which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The ServiceInterfaces are used as the row elements and the Resources are used as the column elements. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating P1 Actual table from P1 Typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Service Provision matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Service Interface
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Measurement Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement
- Actual Property Set
- Forecast
- Protocol
- Standard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438421 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01083: (2026x) P2 Resource Structure

- page_id: `254438421`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438421/2026x+P2+Resource+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:12.596+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p2-resource-structure', 'p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P2 Resource Structure view addresses the composition and (high-level) interaction of resources.

The P2 view links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space='']. The P2 view may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''] view(i.e. in a to-be architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource view suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space='']view may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the P2 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The P2 view can be used to specify typical (or template) organization structures, and also identify how human resources interact with each other and with systems.

This view can provide a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The P2 view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P3 Resource Connectivity' space=''] view.

Resources may be decomposed in the P2 view to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition that the architect sees fit. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''] view may well be the logical representation of the resource shown in the P2 view.

The P2 view may be used for:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- Human – System interactions.
- Typical Organization structures.
- Interface requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability configuration definition).

**Implementation**

The P2 view can be represented using:

- A P2 Resource Structure diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2.png']
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The P2 view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view
