<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438432 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01084: (2026x) P3 Resource Connectivity

- page_id: `254438432`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438432/2026x+P3+Resource+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:15.881+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p3-resource-connectivity']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P3 Resource Connectivity view describes communications networks and pathways that link communications systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The purpose of P3 view is to provide a comprehensive specification of how systems are connected, what interfaces each system exposes (ports), the hardware interface used and the protocols transmitted across the interface.

The networks and pathways documented through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L3 Node Interactions' space='']view.

The P3 view focuses on the physical characteristics of each link by specifying attributes. An example of this might be the geographic location and layout of network components such as routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters.

**Implementation**

The P3 view can be represented using:

- A P3 Resource Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3.PNG']
- A P3 Resource Role Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3_role_connectivity.png']
- A P3 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P3 table' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy
- System
- Function
- Resource Role

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438410 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01085: (2026x) P4 Resource Functions

- page_id: `254438410`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438410/2026x+P4+Resource+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:11.358+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p4-resource-functions', 'p4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P4 Resource Functions view addresses resource (human and non-human) functions – i.e. the activities performed by Resources.

The primary purposes of the P4 view are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e. that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.
- Provide implementation-specific realizations of the operational activities specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] view.

The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of data between functions.

The P4 view is the systems view counterpart to the Activity Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space='']) of the operational view.

The P4 view may be used for:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The P4 view can be represented using:

- A P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A P4 Resource Functions diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='P4_matrix.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Standard Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Function Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The P4 Resource Functions view addresses resource (human and non-human) functions – i.e. the activities performed by Resources.</p><p>The primary purposes of the P4 view are to:</p><ul><li>Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.</li><li>Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e. that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).</li><li>Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.</li><li>Provide implementation-specific realizations of the operational activities specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4 - Logical Activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</li></ul><p>The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:</p><ul><li>Allocation of functions to resources.</li><li>Flow of data between functions.</li></ul><p>The P4 view is the systems view counterpart to the Activity Model (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) of the operational view.</p><p>The P4 view may be used for:</p><ul><li>Description of task workflow.</li><li>Identification of functional system requirements.</li><li>Functional decomposition of systems.</li><li>Relate human and system functions.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The P4 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram" ac:title="P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P4_resource_functions_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P4 Resource Functions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>A P4 Resource Functions diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="P4 Resource Functions diagram" ac:title="P4 Resource Functions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P4_resource_functions.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P4 Resource Functions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>A L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix" ac:title="P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P4_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P4 Resource Functions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ebb1739e-4246-43a6-82f1-bedfb338c803"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ebb1739e-4246-43a6-82f1-bedfb338c803">A UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ebb1739e-4246-43a6-82f1-bedfb338c803">A UML Activity diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br style="color: rgb(255,0,0);" /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ebb1739e-4246-43a6-82f1-bedfb338c803">A <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439499 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01086: (2026x) P5 Resource States

- page_id: `254439499`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439499/2026x+P5+Resource+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:12.494+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p5-resource-states', 'p5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P5 Resource States view describes how Resources change state in response to events and other stimuli. The P5 view identifies the states a Resource can be, the allowable changes between those states, and the triggers that cause the state changes.

The P5 view may be used to:

- Define states, events and state transitions (behavioural modelling).
- Identify constraints (input to System Requirements Document).

**Implementation**

The P5 view can be represented using a P5 Resource States diagram which is based on the UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438490 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01087: (2026x) P6 Resource Sequence

- page_id: `254438490`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438490/2026x+P6+Resource+Sequence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:23.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p6-resource-sequence', 'p6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P6 Resource Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between Resources. These are usually depicted as event-trace diagrams and will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

P6 products are valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

The P6 view may be used for:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements (input to System Requirement Document).

**Implementation**

The P6 view can be represented using a P6 Resource Sequence diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P6 diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Role
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438485 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01088: (2026x) P7 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `254438485`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438485/2026x+P7+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:22.489+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p7-physical-data-model', 'p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P7 Physical Data Model view defines the structure of data used by the Resources in the architecture. It is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L7 Logical Data Model' space='']L7 Logical Data Model is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

The P7 view can be represented using:

- A P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P7.png']

- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Exchange Element
- Details
- Physical Data Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438497 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01089: (2026x) P8 Resource Constraints

- page_id: `254438497`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438497/2026x+P8+Resource+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:27.081+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p8-resource-constraints', 'p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P8 Resource Constraints view specifies functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the Resource viewpoint). It constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Resource architecture. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

The P8 view may be used to:

- Define implementation logic.
- Identify resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The P8 view can be represented using:

- A P8 Resource Constraints table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P8.PNG']

- A P8 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P8 table' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

1100550585

1100550587

1100550586

##### Description

UPDM: A type of a human being that is recognized by law as the subject of rights and duties. This is used to define the characteristics that require capturing for ActualPersons (e.g., properties such as address, rank, telephone number, etc.).

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

## PAGE 01094: (2026x) Physical Data Model

- page_id: `254438738`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438738/2026x+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:52.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

476178045

476178048

476178046

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalDataModel is an implementable specification of a data structure. A PhysicalDataModel realizes a LogicalDataModel, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues whilst still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical model.

DoDAF: A Physical Data Model defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[DIV-3 Physical Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-11 Systems Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSV-11+Systems+Data+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438408 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01095: (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint

- page_id: `254438408`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438408/2026x+Physical+Resource+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:11.112+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['physical-resource-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Physical Resource viewpoint is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. This viewpoint associates systems resources to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logical viewpoint' space=''] and/or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Service viewpoint' space='']. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Service viewpoint' space=''], and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logical viewpoint' space=''].

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Service viewpoint" /></ac:link>, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Logical viewpoint" /></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100808206

1100808208

1100808207

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Post (MODAF::PostType, NAF::PostType) is a type of point of contact or responsible person. Note that this is the type of post, e.g., Desk Officer, Commander Land Component, etc.

DoDAF: A Post (DoDAF::PersonType) is a category of persons defined by the role or roles they share that are relevant to an architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 01097: (2026x) Pr Configuration Management

- page_id: `254438469`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438469/2026x+Pr+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:20.309+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['pr-configuration-management', 'pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Pr Configuration Management view depicts the whole lifecycle view of a Resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The Pr view provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of different versions of Resources (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.

The Pr view may be used for:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Pr view can be represented using:

- A Pr Configuration Management diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Pr MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Pr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Resources in Pr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing Resources from Pr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Actual Project Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439089 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01098: (2026x) Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `254439089`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439089/2026x+Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:37.166+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Coverage Analysis
- labels: ['metric-suites', 'capabilities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-opactivities', 'nodes-covered-by-sysresources', 'opactivitie-covered-by-functions', 'opactivities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-sysinterfaces', 'capabilities-covered-by-capconfiguration', 'capabilities-covered-by-projects', 'orgresources-covered-by-projects', 'projects-covered-by-milestones', 'functions-covered-by-sysresources']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.

For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Capabilities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by OpActivities**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by OpActivities" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Nodes Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Nodes Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Functions**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Functions" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OrgResources Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "OrgResources Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Projects Covered by Milestones**

The Metric Suite "Projects Covered by Milestones" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Functions Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Functions Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.</p><p>For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Metric Suite hierarchy" ac:title="Metric Suite hierarchy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_suite_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by OpActivities</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by OpActivities&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Nodes Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Nodes Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Functions</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Functions&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OrgResources Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OrgResources Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Projects Covered by Milestones</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Projects Covered by Milestones&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Functions Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Functions Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439701 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01099: (2026x) Predefined Relation Maps

- page_id: `254439701`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439701/2026x+Predefined+Relation+Maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:30.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Supportive Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are as follows:

##### Capability Structural Map

A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)
- related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, Software System Resources

A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Behavioral Map

A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related Operational Activities
- related System Functions

A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Map

The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Organization Structure Map

The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### System Resource Map

The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.

##### Variant Map

The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p><p>The predefined relation maps are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2c7cce1-4f9b-4604-9e6e-3ac3b551926b" /></p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li>the Capability itself</li><li>related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)</li><li><span>related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, </span>Software System Resources</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li><span>the Capability itself</span></li><li>related Operational Activities</li><li>related System Functions</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.</span></p><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p><span>The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</span></p><h3>System Resource Map</h3><p><span>The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.</span></p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p><span>The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01100: (2026x) Problem Domain

- page_id: `254439502`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439502/2026x+Problem+Domain
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:40.568+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100860662

1100860664

1100860663

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The boundary containing those Nodes which may be realized by functional resources specified in SV-1. There may be more than one alternative solution for a given ProblemDomain specified as a set of SV suites. There may be only one ProblemDomain in a LogicalArchitecture.

DoDAF: NA – covered by the more general temporalWholePart element.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66367f8f-96e3-44cc-af8e-2eb310690dcb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100860662</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb6ef0e-7d67-4984-ac79-72d9cba4c5ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100860664</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c42d461d-9fac-40bc-939c-a0e6937bf400"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100860663</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: The boundary containing those Nodes which may be realized by functional resources specified in SV-1. There may be more than one alternative solution for a given ProblemDomain specified as a set of SV suites. There may be only one ProblemDomain in a LogicalArchitecture.</span></p><p><span>DoDAF: NA – covered by the more general temporalWholePart element.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Property</span></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description">MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description">NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439815 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01102: (2026x) Producing and Consuming Activities dialog

- page_id: `254439815`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439815/2026x+Producing+and+Consuming+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:38.535+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-activities-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog allows you to specify or modify producing and consuming Activities for a selected Operational Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Operational Exchange, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Activity or Consuming Activity property value cell.
- In the OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Operational Activity cell.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog : - Click the **Edit** (...) button in the selected **Producing Operational Activity** or **Consuming Operational Activity** cell. - Click the **Activities** button.

Elements of the dialog are described in the following tables.

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update Diagrams | Select to update all related diagrams. Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular operational exchange as a triangle on every Operational Activity Edge connecting producing and consuming activity actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Activities does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (DoDAF), OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (MODAF), or NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Activity actions and the Operational Exchange will be represented in this new diagram. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Activities. |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Click to show views containing specified activities (see the following figure).Click once again to hide this information. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify a pair of producing and consuming activities

1. In the Producing Operational Activity column of a selected row, create a new value:
  - Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available activities and select one. If you do not see the list of available functions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the**Performed Activities** button.
  - Click the “ ... ” button to create a new or select an existing activity from the model. The Select Operational Activity dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create activities.
2. Perform the same action described above for the **Consuming Operational Activity** column of the same row.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering Operational Activities and Functions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular operational exchange as a triangle on every Operational Activity Edge connecting producing and consuming activity actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Activities does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (DoDAF), OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (MODAF), or NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Activity actions and the Operational Exchange will be represented in this new diagram.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Add</td><td><p>Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Activities.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Remove From Table</td><td>Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Full Paths</td><td><p>Click to show views containing specified activities (see the following figure).</p><p>Click once again to hide this information.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:alt="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" ac:title="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Activities dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_full_types_button_in_producing_consuming_activities_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To specify a pair of producing and consuming activities</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the Producing Operational Activity column of a selected row, create a new value:</p><ul><li><p>Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available activities and select one.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="017e31a3-8710-4745-b34e-af7b16e45360" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the list of available functions, click <ac:image ac:alt="Filter Options" ac:title="Filter Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select  the<strong> Performed Activities</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the “<strong>...</strong>” button to create a new or select an existing activity from the model. The <strong>Select Operational Activity</strong> dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create activities.</li></ul></li><li><p> Perform the same action described above for the <strong>Consuming Operational Activity</strong> column of the same row.</p></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="6155de8f-0747-45ec-a574-288c84d1be43" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering Operational Activities and Functions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439824 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01103: (2026x) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog

- page_id: `254439824`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439824/2026x+Producing+and+Consuming+Functions+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:39.924+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-functions-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to specify or modify producing and consuming Functions for a selected Resource Interaction.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Interaction, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Function or Consuming Function cell.
- In the SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Function or Consuming Function cell.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager dialog** : - Click the **Edit** (...) button in the selected **Producing Function or Consuming Function** cell. - Click the **Functions** button.

The **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog is embedded in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizardas a step #2.

Elements of the dialog are described in the following tables.

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update Diagrams | Select to update all related diagrams. Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular resource interaction as a triangle on every Function Edge connecting producing and consuming function actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Functions does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new SV-4 System Functionality Description Flow (DoDAF), SV-4 Functionality Description Flow (MODAF), or NSV-4 System Functionality Description Flow (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Function actions and the Resource Interaction will be represented in this new diagram. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Functions. |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Click to show views containing specified functions (see the following figure).Click once again to hide this information. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify a pair of producing and consuming functions

1. In the**Producing Function** column of a selected row, create a new value:
  - Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available functions and select one. If you do not see the list of available functions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Performed Functions** button.
  - Click the “ ... ” button to create a new or select a function existing in the model. The Select Function dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create functions.
2. Perform the same action described above for the **Consuming Function** column of the same row.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering Operational Activities and Functions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1338991974

1338991976

1338991975

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Project (MODAF::ProjectType, NAF::ProjectType) is used to define a category of project: For example, “Program”, “Acquisition Project” or “Training Program”.

DoDAF: NA (only Individual Project in DoDAF).

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

- page_id: `254439183`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1339329081

1339329084

1339329083

##### Description

UPDM: An element representing a collection of themes (e.g., DLOD or DOTMLPF) which is connected to a Project as part of a Project’s definition. This is used as a template for ActualProjectMilestones.

MODAF/NAF: An event in a Project by which progress is measured.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that one ActualProject (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project) follows from another, i.e. the target ActualProject cannot start until the source ActualProject has ended.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1343230441

1343230443

1343230442

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A ProjectStatus (MODAF::StatusAtMilestone, NAF::StatusAtMilestone) is a relationship between a Status and a milestone that asserts the status (i.e., level of progress) of a ProjectTheme for the project at the time of the ActualProjectMilestone (MODAF::Milestone, NAF::Milestone).

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Slot

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439856 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01111: (2026x) Project Status Application Wizard

- page_id: `254439856`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439856/2026x+Project+Status+Application+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:42.558+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['project-status-application-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Project Status Application Wizard** is used to apply the DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or custom Milestone type to the selected element, which can be one of the following:

- Out Of Service Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project Milestone

To open the **Project Status Application Wizard**

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The Project Status Application Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of two steps:

1. Select an Actual Project Milestone Type. How to select an Actual Project Milestone Type?In this step, select DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or create your own Milestone type.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify Project Status at a particular Milestone. How to specify Project Status at a particular Milestone?In this step, specify project threads and status indicators of the selected Actual Project Milestone Type.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Project threadsStatus indicators**DLOD Milestone type**EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTrainingDLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues[IMAGE alt='' src='']Project threadsStatus indicators**DOTMLPF Milestone type**DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilitiesMateriel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & SupportIf you selected your own milestone type, you can create your own projects threads and status indicators.To create project threads and status indicators1. In the Project Thread area, click the **Add** button.2. Specify the thread name.3. In the Status Indicator area, click the **Add** button.4. Specify the indicator name.5. Click the **Fill Color** button.6. In the **Color dialog** choose the color and click **OK**.To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone**button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.To remove the applied Project Status1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the right bottom corner.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying Project Status to Lr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying Project Status in PV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing Project Status from Lr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing Project Status from PV-2 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

1343252265

1343252267

1343252266

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An aspect by which the progress of various Projects may be measured. In UK MOD, this could be one of the defense lines of development (DLOD), or DOTMLPF in the US.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

1339030543

1339030545

1339030544

##### Description

MODAF: A Project (MODAF::ProjectType, NAF::ProjectType) is used to define a category of project: For example, “Programme”, “Acquisition Project” or “Training Programme”.

DoDAF: NA (only Individual Project in DoDAF).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 01114: (2026x) Project viewpoint

- page_id: `254438387`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438387/2026x+Project+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:09.282+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['project-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416628093

1416628095

1416628094

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Project Viewpoint describe how programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to them, and dependencies between them. Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

1416628092

**Related Pages:**

## PAGE 01118: (2026x) Provides Competence

- page_id: `254439604`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439604/2026x+Provides+Competence
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:27.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100880881

1100880883

1100880882

##### Description

UPDM: Asserts that a Resource type provides a competence.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole).

DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

[NSV-1 System Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) NSV-1+System+Interface+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438964 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01119: (2026x) Publishing project as web portal

- page_id: `254438964`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438964/2026x+Publishing+project+as+web+portal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:26.060+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Reports
- labels: ['interactive-web-portal', 'publish-project-as-web-portal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Publish your project as interactive web portal and share it with stakeholders, including even those incapable of reading models in MagicDraw! The published web portal eliminates less needed elements from the model tree and proposes the most relevant views of enterprise architecture project, such as capability/strategic, operational, systems, services, standards, projects. This allows you to discover your project in a user-friendly and easily browsable environment.

If you want to publish a project as a web portal, you must install the Document Modeling Plugin. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='NMDOC']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also provide feedback on any element or diagram while reviewing an Enterprise Architecture Portal report of the project.

Use quick search to find elements and diagrams for viewing their custom contents and share this information with colleagues.

Publishing the project as a web portal is as easy as generating any report.

To publish a project

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard .
2. Select one of the following templates:
  - DoDAF > DoDAF Web Report
  - MODAF > MODAF Web Report
  - NAF > NAF Web Report
  - NAF 4 > NAF 4.0 Web Report
3. Follow the steps of the Report Wizard .
4. Place the output in a shared directory or on web server to make it available for co-workers.

The following table introduces what you can expect in your web report. In every view of the report, you may find the following information:

- What elements are collected
- Collected element properties
- Related elements
- Related diagram types

| Capability View – Enterprise Phases |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enterprise Phase properties | Related elements | Related diagrams |
| IdentifierNameStart dateEnd dateDocumentation | GoalsCapabilitiesReferences | Relation Map |
| Capability View – Capabilities |  |  |
| Capability properties | Related elements | Related diagrams |
| IdentifierNameDocumentation | Composed of CapabilitiesExhibited by Enterprise PhasesExhibited by NodesExhibited by Operational ActivitiesExhibited by ResourcesExhibited by FunctionsMeasurementsReferences | Capability Behavioral MapCapability Structural MapRelation Map |
| Operational View – Nodes |  |  |
| Node properties | Related elements | Related diagrams |
| IdentifierNameDocumentation | Node PortsNode RolesOperational ExchangesExhibits CapabilitiesPerforms Operational ActivitiesImplemented by SystemResourcesMeasurementsConstraintsReferences | OV-2 / NOV-2 InternalOV-6b / NOV-6b State diagramsOV-6c / NOV-6c Interaction diagramsImplementation MapNode Impact Analysis MapRelation Map |

| Operational View – Operational Exchanges |  |  |

| Systems View – Resources |  |  |

| IdentifierNameDocumentation | Resource PortsResource RolesResource InteractionsPerforms FunctionsExhibits CapabilitiesImplements NodesMilestonesStandards and PoliciesMeasurementsConstraintsReferences | SV-1 / NSV-1 InternalSV-2 / NSV-2 InternalSV-10b / NSV-10b State diagramsSV-10c / NSV-10c Interaction diagramsVariant MapSystem Resource MapResource Impact Analysis MapRelation Map |
| Systems View – Functions |  |  |
| Function properties | Related elements | Related diagrams |

| IdentifierNameDocumentation | Resource PortsResource RolesInterfacesResource InteractionsPerforms FunctionsImplements NodesExhibits CapabilitiesProject ResourcesMilestonesStandards and PoliciesMeasurementsConstraintsReferences | SvcV-2 InternalSvcV-10b State diagramsSvcV-10c Interaction diagramsRelation Map |

| IdentifierNameDocumentation | InterfacesService AttributesService OperationsService Message HandlersExposes CapabilitiesMeasurementsConstraintsReferences | SOV-4b / NSOV-4b State diagramsSOV-4c / NSOV-4c Interaction diagramsRelation Map |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438389 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01120: (2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships

- page_id: `254438389`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438389/2026x+PV-1+Project+Portfolio+Relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:09.431+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-1', 'pv-1-project-portfolio-relationships', 'pv-1-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29186712

29186714

29186713

**Description**

The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.

The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

PV-1 can be represented using:

- A PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='PV-1_diagram.png']
- A PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='AcV-1_PV-1_matrix.PNG']

The model is strongly linked with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-4 Capability Dependencies' space=''] model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.

29186711

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438393 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01121: (2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines

- page_id: `254438393`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438393/2026x+PV-2+Project+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:09.713+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-2', 'pv-2-project-timelines', 'pv-2-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29712997

29712999

29712998

**Description**

The PV-2 provides a timeline perspective on programs. The PV-2 is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of DoDD 5000.1 Defense Acquisition System policies to achieve a successfully integrated capability. The PV-2 is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the PV-2 includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

PV-2 can be represented using a PV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use of PV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] model, if one exists.

29712996
