<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438396 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01122: (2026x) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `254438396`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438396/2026x+PV-3+Project+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:09.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-3', 'pv-3-project-to-capability-mapping', 'pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35598491

35598493

35598492

**Description**

The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space=''], but provides the interface between Capability and Project Models rather than Operational to System Models.

35598490

**Related Pages:**

****

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity Part of Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity Part of Project' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89ef814d-ddb3-448c-979c-3740375377f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35598491</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1b2089c-acb7-4da4-bd0f-0e8cfebe098a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35598493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60e40f72-61ed-4847-b9b2-abe74825a712"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35598492</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.</p><p>The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to projects.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ac:alt="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-3.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but provides the interface between Capability and Project Models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d57e193e-b0ed-4ad1-93ad-1307256cae58"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35598490</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2aa70e64-ec49-49fb-843d-7d0b9b529a0a" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activity Part of Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Capability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activity Part of Project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438888 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01123: (2026x) Question dialog

- page_id: `254438888`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438888/2026x+Question+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:21.782+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks
- labels: ['question-dialog', 'convert-diagram-types', 'convert-element-types', 'convert-template-structure', 'display-project-conversion-confirmation-question']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Question** dialog opens when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space=''].

The dialog asks whether user wants to convert the model according to specified options or not.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Check box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert diagram types | Select to convert all diagram types to corresponding diagram types in the selected framework. |
| Convert element types | Select to convert all element types to their aliases in the selected framework. |
| Convert template structure | Select to convert the template structure to the template structure in the selected framework. |
| Do not show this message again | Select to hide the dialog the next time when performing the model conversion.To restore the usage of the Question dialogClick Options > Environment.In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.In the General properties, set the Display project conversion confirmation question property value to true. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Question</strong> dialog opens when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[converting a model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The dialog asks whether user wants to convert the model according to specified options or not.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Question dialog" ac:title="Question dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Question dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Convert diagram types</td><td><p>Select to convert all diagram types to corresponding diagram types in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Convert element types</td><td><p>Select to convert all element types to their aliases in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Convert template structure</td><td><p>Select to convert the template structure to the template structure in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Do not show this message again</p></td><td><p>Select to hide the dialog the next time when performing the model conversion.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong>Question</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM </strong>options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Display project conversion confirmation question</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439862 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01124: (2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard

- page_id: `254439862`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439862/2026x+Realized+Operational+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:43.188+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-operational-exchange-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard gives the instructions to follow, when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges to:

- A Needline between a pair of Performer usages in OV-2 (NOV-2 in NAF) diagrams.
- An Operational Activity Edge between a pair of Operational Activities in OV-5 (NOV-5 in NAF) behavioral diagram.
- An Operational Message between a pair of two lifelines of Performers in OV-6c (NOV-6c in NAF) diagram.

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard includes of two steps:

1. Operational Exchange creation (mandatory). How to create a new or select an existing Operational Exchange?This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Performers, and a direction of a Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Performers are known or not, requested information differs: 
- If sending and receiving Performers are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']- If sending and receiving Performers are known, they are added to a **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']BoxDescriptionConveyed ItemOne or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Performers to receiving Performers.Operational ExchangeOperational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Performers are known from the wizard invocation context.Sending PerformerPerformer that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.Receiving PerformerPerformer that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.DirectionDirection of the information flow. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Performance is switched with the receiving Performance. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly.Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationshipsSelect the check box, if you want to add the Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams.ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Conveyed Item** box)Click to open the **Select Conveyed Information** dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Sending Performer**box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Receiving****Performer**box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.
2. Producing and Consuming Activities specification (optional). How to specify Producing and Consuming Activities?This step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='']
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard gives the instructions to follow, when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges to:</p><ul><li>A Needline between a pair of Performer usages in OV-2 (NOV-2 in NAF) diagrams.</li><li>An Operational Activity Edge between a pair of Operational Activities in OV-5 (NOV-5 in NAF) behavioral diagram.</li><li>An Operational Message between a pair of two lifelines of Performers in OV-6c (NOV-6c in NAF) diagram.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard includes of two steps:</p><ol><li><p>Operational Exchange creation (mandatory).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="651cd12a-77f7-4e5d-82b6-54f2c5bdfeb9" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create a new or select an existing Operational Exchange?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Performers, and a direction of a Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Performers are known or not, requested information differs:<br />- If sending and receiving Performers are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step1_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>- If sending and receiving Performers are known, they are added to a <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><table><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Conveyed Item</td><td><p>One or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Performers to receiving Performers.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Operational Exchange</td><td><p>Operational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.</p><p>If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="8d1d0bf6-f972-4359-894c-7aba5727d5bf" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if sending and receiving Performers are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Sending Performer</td><td><p>Performer that sends the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1121be01-02da-4a03-9cfd-593c174eff13" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Receiving Performer</td><td><p> Performer that receives the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eeb42d77-7eb4-49a3-bb3b-0c44d2bd70f7" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td> Direction</td><td><p> Direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying the direction" ac:title="Specifying the direction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_direction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Performance is switched with the receiving Performance. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships</p></td><td><p>Select the check box, if you want to add the Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Conveyed Item</strong> box)</p></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Select Conveyed Information</strong> dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Sending Performer </strong>box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Receiving </strong><strong>Performer </strong>box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Producing and Consuming Activities specification (optional).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cb855e5b-1d49-429d-bd9a-fb7521c86700" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify Producing and Consuming Activities?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Performers.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Producing and Consuming Activities in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Specifying Producing and Consuming Activities in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="8d4d74bb-45d8-4a40-8144-76474ed50c24" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439871 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 01125: (2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard

- page_id: `254439871`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439871/2026x+Realized+Resource+Interaction+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:43.832+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-resource-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard provides you with the instructions to follow when adding a new Resource Interaction to:

- A Resource Interface between a pair of Resource Roles in the SV-1, SV-2 diagrams.
- A Function Edge (Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow) between a pair of Functions in the SV-4 Flows diagram.
- A Resource Message between a pair of two lifelines of Resources in the SV-10c diagram.

The **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard includes of two steps:

1. Resource Interaction creation (mandatory). How to create new or select an existing Resource Interaction?This step is designed to create a new Resource Interaction by specifying a conveyed Resource Interaction Item, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource Interaction. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs: 
- If sending and receiving Resources are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']- If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']BoxDescriptionConveyed ItemOne or more Resource Interaction Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.Resource InteractionResource Interaction that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Resource Interaction from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.Sending ResourceResource that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.Receiving ResourceResource that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.DirectionDirection of the information flow. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource Interaction will be changed accordingly.Add Resource Interaction to corresponding relationshipsSelect the check box, if you want to add the Resource Interaction to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. These are the relationships that can realize the Resource Interaction:- System Connector (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)- Resource Connector (DoDAF) / Resource Interface (MODAF / NAF) (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)- Function Edge (SV-4 Systems Functionality Description)- Resource Message (SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description)ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Conveyed Item** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Sending Resource** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Receiving Resource** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.
2. step2Producing and Consuming Functions specification (optional). Click here for detailed descriptionThis step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource Interaction. Resource Interactions are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Resource (System Function (DoDAF) or Function (MODAF / NAF)). In terms of UPDM, Resources perform System Functions (DoDAF) or Functions (MODAF / NAF).[IMAGE alt='' src='']There should always be a pair of functions: one to produce and other to consume information flow

To open the **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard do one of the following

- Create the Resource Interaction relationship between System Resources. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Communications DescriptionSV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-1 System Interface DescriptionNSV-2 System Communications DescriptionNSV-4 System Functionality Description
- Select a Resource Connector or a Resource Interface and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description
- On the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Resource Connector or a Resource Interface. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description
- Select a Function Edge and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Function Edge. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- On the table toolbar, click the**Add New** button. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- Select a Resource Message and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description**NAF:**NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Resource Message. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='']**MODAF:**SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description**NAF:**NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P6 diagram' space='']
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-10c diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard provides you with the instructions to follow when adding a new Resource Interaction to:</p><ul><li>A Resource Interface between a pair of Resource Roles in the SV-1, SV-2 diagrams.</li><li>A Function Edge (Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow) between a pair of Functions in the SV-4 Flows diagram.</li><li>A Resource Message between a pair of two lifelines of Resources in the SV-10c diagram.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard includes of two steps:</p><ol><li><p>Resource Interaction creation (mandatory).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="95f15a0d-8e2b-4c2d-a29e-77cb4d05975c" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create new or select an existing Resource Interaction?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to create a new Resource Interaction by specifying a conveyed Resource Interaction Item, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource Interaction. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs:<br />- If sending and receiving Resources are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating a new or selecting an existing Resource Interaction in Realized Resource Interaction wizard" ac:title="Creating a new or selecting an existing Resource Interaction in Realized Resource Interaction wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_interaction_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>- If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a <strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating a new or selecting an existing Resource Interaction in Realized Resource Interaction wizard" ac:title="Creating a new or selecting an existing Resource Interaction in Realized Resource Interaction wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_interaction_wizard_step1_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><table><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Conveyed Item</td><td><p>One or more Resource Interaction Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Resource Interaction</td><td><p>Resource Interaction that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.</p><p>If you select an existing Resource Interaction from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="6c06d46f-ef6c-4bc3-8a6f-e1f39f50bad9" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Sending Resource</td><td><p>Resource that sends the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="aed8c97e-ac9c-4be5-8268-d42f8d356884" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Receiving Resource</td><td><p> Resource that receives the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="76af2f37-0a3e-4f3d-9767-3ca80bd6b39d" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td> Direction</td><td><p> Direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_interaction_wizard_direction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource Interaction will be changed accordingly.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add Resource Interaction to corresponding relationships</p></td><td><p>Select the check box, if you want to add the Resource Interaction to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. These are the relationships that can realize the Resource Interaction:</p><p>- System Connector (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)</p><p>- Resource Connector (DoDAF) / Resource Interface (MODAF / NAF) (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)</p><p>- Function Edge (SV-4 Systems Functionality Description)</p><p>- Resource Message (SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description)</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Conveyed Item</strong> box)</p></td><td><p>Click to open the element Selection dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Sending Resource</strong> box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Receiving Resource</strong> box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f7bc95ab-43e0-4195-9bc7-f6e633ce251f" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">step2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Producing and Consuming Functions specification (optional).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="96c9abcb-95c7-485a-8ed1-a97a59b4f598" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here for detailed description</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource Interaction. Resource Interactions are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Resource (System Function (DoDAF) or Function (MODAF / NAF)). In terms of UPDM, Resources perform System Functions (DoDAF) or Functions (MODAF / NAF).</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Producing and Consuming Functions in Realized Resource Interaction wizard" ac:title="Specifying Producing and Consuming Functions in Realized Resource Interaction wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_interaction_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cf023240-a058-4e76-bef3-fde3d9e11f62" ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There should always be a pair of functions: one to produce and other to consume information flow</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To open the <strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Create the Resource Interaction relationship between System Resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="20e5b251-7b1d-4d7c-834b-bdf15931a28f" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to see the views for which this action is available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>DoDAF:</strong></p><p><ac:link><ri:page Specification</p><p>SV-2 Resource Communications Description</p><p>SV-4 Functionality Description</p><p><strong>NAF:</strong></p><p>NSV-1 System Interface Description</p><p>NSV-2 System Communications <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438524 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 01126: (2026x) Relating Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `254438524`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438524/2026x+Relating+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:32.753+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['relate-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate projects

1. Select two Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between projects

1. Select two related Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-2_Validation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relating Projects in Lr diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-2_Validation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relating Projects in PV-2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439168 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->

To delete a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

## PAGE 01129: (2026x) Removing Project Status from Lr diagram

- page_id: `254438613`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438613/2026x+Removing+Project+Status+from+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1

- labels: ['remove-project-status-from-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. In the right bottom corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

1106364366

1106364368

1106364367

##### Description

A RequestPoint models the use of a service by a participant and defines the connection point through which a Participant makes requests and uses or consumes services.

##### Origin

SoaML

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

## PAGE 01137: (2026x) Requires Competence

- page_id: `254439611`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439611/2026x+Requires+Competence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:25.837+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100968495

1100968497

1100968496

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole, NAF::CompetenceForRole).

DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1e88d58-fe0e-4816-8901-a0a07c89c981"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100968495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce51aee2-ccde-44b4-b530-76cbaba84e77"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100968497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef74012d-3843-426a-8a12-56e0496d63d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100968496</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole, NAF::CompetenceForRole).</span></p><p><span>DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Dependency</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 01138: (2026x) Resource Artifact

- page_id: `254438736`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438736/2026x+Resource+Artifact
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:53.117+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

315044503

315044505

315044504

##### Description

UPDM: A combination of physical element, energy, and data that are combined used to accomplish a task or function.

MODAF/NAF: A type of man-made object. Examples are “car”, “radio”, “fuel”, etc. (MODAF::Artifact, NAF::Artifact).

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| facilityType : String[0..*] | The type of facility that the artifact is associated with. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

- page_id: `254438761`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

506464327

506464329

506464328

##### Description

UPDM: A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a connection exists between two ports belonging to parts in a system composite structure model (MODAF::SystemPortConnector, NAF::SystemPortConnector).

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Connector

##### Related MODAF view

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

[MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description)

[NSV-2 System Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSV-2+System+Communications+Description)

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554687045

554687047

554687046

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A rule governing the structural or functional aspects of an implementation - this may also include constraints on OrganizationalResources that are part of an implementation.

DoDAF: The range of permissible states for an object (DoDAF::Constraint).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| InformationTechnologyStandardCategory : String[*] | The information technology standard category that is associated with the resource constraint. |

##### Extensions

UML Constraint

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-10a Systems Rules Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model)

554790083

554790085

554790084

##### Description

UPDM: ResourceInteraction represents data that is exchanged between the resources.

MODAF/NAF: An assertion that two FunctionalResources interact. For example, data exchange between systems, conversations between people, people using systems.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Information Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439880 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01143: (2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog

- page_id: `254439880`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439880/2026x+Resource+Interaction+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:44.219+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['resource-interaction-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Interaction Manager dialog enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data. You can edit existing Resource Interactions by adding new Resource Interaction Items, realizing or ignoring existing Resource Interactions on selected relationship, specifying producing and consuming Functions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Resource Interaction Manager** dialog

1. Select Resource Interface, Resource Connector, Association, Function Edge , or Resource Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction_manager.png'] . The Resource Interaction Manager dialog opens.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P6 diagram' space='']

- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-10c diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram
